ConstructorArguments: Clears the current input actions, so that none of them are active. Unlike ClearActions this will unpress the buttons, so they won't be active again until they're pressed again.

Documentation.Summary:
Clears the current input actions, so that none of them are active. Unlike ClearActions
this will unpress the buttons, so they won't be active again until they're pressed again.

ConstructorArguments: Releases the action, and it won't be active again until it's pressed again.

ConstructorArguments: Finds the InputAction.GroupName of the given action.

Params.action: Action name to find the group name of.

ConstructorArguments:
Returns the name of a key bound to this InputAction For example: 
Input.GetButtonOrigin( "Undo" )
could return SPACE if using keyboard or A Button when using a controller.

Documentation.Summary: Returns the name of a key bound to this InputAction For example: Input.GetButtonOrigin( "Undo" ) could return SPACE if using keyboard or A Button when using a controller.

ConstructorArguments: Get a glyph texture from the controller bound to the action. If no binding is found will return an 'UNBOUND' glyph.

Documentation.Summary:
Get a glyph texture from the controller bound to the action.
If no binding is found will return an 'UNBOUND' glyph.
Documentation.Remarks: You should update your UI with this every frame, it's very cheap to call and context can change.

ConstructorArguments: Get a glyph texture from an analog input on a controller.

Documentation.Summary: Get a glyph texture from an analog input on a controller.

ConstructorArguments:
Returns the name of the analog axis bound to this InputAnalog. For example: 
Input.GetButtonOrigin( InputAnalog.Move )
could return Left Joystick

Documentation.Summary: Returns the name of the analog axis bound to this . For example: Input.GetButtonOrigin( InputAnalog.Move ) could return Left Joystick

ConstructorArguments: Trigger a haptic event on supported controllers including Xbox trigger impulse rumble.

Documentation.Summary: Trigger a haptic event on supported controllers including Xbox trigger impulse rumble.
Documentation.Remarks: SDL will translate these commands into haptic pulses that should work on all controller types.

Params.leftMotor: The speed of the left motor, between 0.0 and 1.0.
Params.rightMotor: The speed of the right motor, between 0.0 and 1.0.
Params.leftTrigger: (Xbox One controller only) The speed of the left trigger motor, between 0.0 and 1.0.
Params.rightTrigger: (Xbox One controller only) The speed of the right trigger motor, between 0.0 and 1.0.
Params.duration: How long (in milliseconds) should we apply this for?

ConstructorArguments: Trigger haptics based on a predefined HapticEffect. All HapticEffects are normalized (start at 0, peak at 1).

Documentation.Summary:
Trigger haptics based on a predefined <see cref="T:Sandbox.HapticEffect" />.
All <see cref="T:Sandbox.HapticEffect" />s are normalized (start at 0, peak at 1).

Params.lengthScale: The amount to scale the pattern's length by.
Params.frequencyScale: The amount to scale the pattern's frequency by.
Params.amplitudeScale: The amount to scale the pattern's amplitude by.

ConstructorArguments: Trigger haptics based on a predefined HapticEffect. All HapticEffects are normalized (start at 0, peak at 1).

Params.frequencyScale: The amount to scale the pattern's frequency by.
Params.amplitudeScale: The amount to scale the pattern's amplitude by.
